block_01: Qualcomm, a multinational semiconductor and telecommunications equipment company that provides chips to companies such as Apple, has been hit by the FTC (Federal Trade Commision)  The FTC took Qualcomm to trial regarding allegations of violating antitrust laws.
block_02: The decision has been made. U.S District Judge Lucy Koh has officially sided with the FTC and stated that Qualcomm “strangled competition” by charging insane and unfair royalty fees.   Qualcomm has been ordered to renegotiate the license agreements that have and cannot only work exclusively with one phone maker.
block_03: This ruling could force Qualcomm to change their business model and most importantly, Qualcomm is required to submit to a watchdog to ensure they are running properly and within the laws for 7 years.
block_04: With potential business changes and monitoring for the next 7 years, how will investors feel about this ruling?
